title: Early binding and late binding

exercises.title: Observe early vs late binding
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal

Demonstrate the behavioral difference between early binding and late binding. A non-virtual function is resolved at compile time (early binding), while a virtual function is resolved at runtime (late binding). Both functions exist on the same class hierarchy.

## Input

- A single integer `n` (1 <= n <= 5), the number of objects to create.

## Output

For each object, print two lines:
1. `Early: Base` (the non-virtual call through `Base&` always resolves to `Base`)
2. `Late: Derived` (the virtual call through `Base&` resolves to `Derived` at runtime)

After all objects, print one final line:
```
Done
```

For input `2`:
```
Early: Base
Late: Derived
Early: Base
Late: Derived
Done
```

## Requirements

- Define a class `Base` with two public const member functions:
  - `std::string_view earlyBound() const` (non-virtual) that returns `"Base"`.
  - `virtual std::string_view lateBound() const` that returns `"Base"`.
- Define a class `Derived` that publicly inherits from `Base` with:
  - `std::string_view earlyBound() const` (non-virtual, hides the base version) that returns `"Derived"`.
  - `std::string_view lateBound() const override` that returns `"Derived"`.
- In `main`, read `n`. For each iteration, create a `Derived` object on the stack. Create a `const Base&` reference to it. Print `Early: ` followed by calling `earlyBound()` through the reference. Print `Late: ` followed by calling `lateBound()` through the reference.
- After the loop, print `Done`.

test_cases.label: Two objects
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Early: Base
Late: Derived
Early: Base
Late: Derived
Done

test_cases.label: Five objects
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Early: Base
Late: Derived
Early: Base
Late: Derived
Early: Base
Late: Derived
Early: Base
Late: Derived
Early: Base
Late: Derived
Done

exercises.title: Polymorphic dispatch with mixed types
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal

Build a small hierarchy with `Instrument` as the base class and two derived classes, `Piano` and `Drum`. Store mixed instruments in an array of base pointers and observe that virtual calls (late binding) dispatch to the correct derived function while non-virtual calls (early binding) always call the base version.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `N` (1 <= N <= 6), the number of instruments.
- Next `N` lines: a single word, either `piano` or `drum`.

## Output

For each instrument, print two lines:
1. `Binding: static` (the non-virtual `bindingType()` through `Instrument*` always returns the base version)
2. `Sound: <sound>` (the virtual `play()` through `Instrument*` returns the derived version)

where `<sound>` is `Plink` for piano and `Boom` for drum.

After all instruments, print:
```
Total: <N>
```

For input `3` / `piano` / `drum` / `piano`:
```
Binding: static
Sound: Plink
Binding: static
Sound: Boom
Binding: static
Sound: Plink
Total: 3
```

## Requirements

- Define a class `Instrument` with:
  - A non-virtual public function `std::string_view bindingType() const` that returns `"static"`.
  - A `virtual` public function `std::string_view play() const` that returns `"..."`. 
  - A `virtual` destructor (use `virtual ~Instrument() = default;`).
- Define `Piano` (publicly derived from `Instrument`) that overrides `play()` to return `"Plink"`. It also has its own non-virtual `bindingType()` that returns `"dynamic"` (this version will never be called through the base pointer, proving early binding).
- Define `Drum` (publicly derived from `Instrument`) that overrides `play()` to return `"Boom"`. It also has its own non-virtual `bindingType()` that returns `"dynamic"`.
- In `main`, read `N`. Use a `std::vector<Instrument*>` to store `N` dynamically allocated instruments. Loop over the vector, calling `bindingType()` and `play()` through the `Instrument*`. Print `Total: <N>`. Delete all instruments.

test_cases.label: Mixed piano and drum
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Binding: static
Sound: Plink
Binding: static
Sound: Boom
Binding: static
Sound: Plink
Total: 3

test_cases.label: All drums
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Binding: static
Sound: Boom
Binding: static
Sound: Boom
Total: 2
